001.comment: Core Anthro – D&D 5e species stats + feral anatomy, body language, speech patterns (24 species). SFW version – no explicit anatomical descriptions.
001.content:
### ANTHRO ANATOMY & SPECIES (D&D 5e BASED) – SFW

**GROUND RULES:** Digitigrade legs for most species (walking on toes). Females have flat chests (non-pregnant, non-nursing). All genitalia retain natural feral form – never humanized.

**GENDER-SPECIFIC RULES:** Use species-appropriate body language and physical descriptions. Never use clinical or human anatomical terms. Describe through posture, movement, fur condition, tail position, ear position, scent, and sound.

---

#### CANID (wolf, fox, dog) – MEDIUM digitigrade
**D&D 5e Stats:** +2 DEX, +1 WIS | Size: Medium | Speed: 30 ft | Darkvision 60 ft
**Traits:** Keen Smell (advantage on Perception checks involving smell), Pack Tactics (advantage on attack rolls if ally within 5 ft), Blood Scent (advantage on tracking wounded creatures)
**Body:** tail up/curved=dominance, wag=joy, tuck=fear; ears fwd=interest, flat=fear/aggression.
**Sounds:** growl, snarl, bark, howl.
**Speech:** short, barking, hard consonants. "Come. Now."
**Speech Tempo:** fast, clipped, impatient. Rarely uses full sentences.

---

#### FELID (cat, tiger, lion) – MEDIUM-LARGE digitigrade
**D&D 5e Stats:** +2 DEX, +1 CHA | Size: Medium or Large | Speed: 30 ft | Darkvision 60 ft
**Traits:** Claws (1d4 + STR slashing, natural weapon), Feline Agility (bonus action, double speed for 1 turn, recharge on short rest), Cat's Grace (advantage on Acrobatics checks)
**Body:** tail slow sway=tension, lash=aggression, straight=friendly; ears fwd=interest, flat=fear.
**Sounds:** purr, meow, hiss, growl, roar.
**Speech:** drawn-out vowels, sibilant. "Yooou are testing me..."
**Speech Tempo:** slow, sibilant, drawn-out. Often purrs or hisses between words.

---

#### URSINE (bear, badger) – LARGE plantigrade
**D&D 5e Stats:** +2 STR, +1 CON | Size: Large | Speed: 30 ft | Darkvision 60 ft
**Traits:** Bear Strength (advantage on Strength checks), Tough Hide (AC 13 + DEX when unarmored), Cold Resistance (resistance to cold damage)
**Body:** ears fwd=interest, flat=fear; fur raise=size increase.
**Sounds:** growl, snort, roar.
**Speech:** growling, deep, short. "Go. Now. Bear angry."
**Speech Tempo:** very slow, rumbling, with long pauses.

---

#### MUSTELID (otter, ferret) – SMALL-MEDIUM digitigrade
**D&D 5e Stats:** +2 DEX, +1 INT | Size: Small or Medium | Speed: 30 ft | Darkvision 60 ft
**Traits:** Slippery (advantage on escape from grapples), Burrow (can burrow through loose earth at 10 ft speed), Aquatic Adaptation (can hold breath 10 minutes)
**Body:** tail constant motion; small round ears.
**Sounds:** chirp (play), hiss (fear), squeal (pain).
**Speech:** fast, chattering, high-pitched. "Look-look-look!"
**Speech Tempo:** extremely fast, chattering, often repeats words or syllables.

---

#### EQUINE (horse, zebra) – LARGE unguligrade
**D&D 5e Stats:** +2 STR, +1 DEX | Size: Large | Speed: 40 ft | Darkvision 60 ft
**Traits:** Hoof Strike (1d6 + STR bludgeoning, natural weapon), Sure-Footed (advantage on saves vs being knocked prone), Long-Distance Runner (travel speed doubled)
**Body:** tail swat=irritation, hang=tired; ears fwd=interest, back=irritation.
**Sounds:** neigh, snort, stamp.
**Speech:** calm, nasal, sometimes snorts. "Easy. Everything's fine."
**Speech Tempo:** calm, measured, with occasional snorts.

---

#### AVIAN (bird, raven, eagle, owl) – MEDIUM digitigrade
**D&D 5e Stats:** +2 DEX, +1 WIS | Size: Medium | Speed: 25 ft, Fly 30 ft | Darkvision 60 ft
**Traits:** Flight (30 ft flying speed, cannot wear heavy armor), Keen Sight (advantage on Perception checks involving sight), Talons (1d4 + DEX slashing, natural weapon)
**Body:** tail feathers fan=excitement, close=fear; fluff=cold/excitement.
**Sounds:** chirp, song, caw, screech.
**Speech:** chirping, precise, repeats last word. "Danger! Danger from west!"
**Speech Tempo:** rapid, staccato, often repeats key words or phrases.

---

#### REPTILE / KOBOLD – MEDIUM (reptile) / SMALL (kobold) digitigrade
**D&D 5e Stats:** +2 CON, +1 INT | Size: Small (kobold) or Medium (reptile) | Speed: 30 ft | Darkvision 60 ft
**Traits:** Scales (AC 13 + DEX when unarmored), Cold-Blooded (vulnerable to cold, resistant to heat), Trap-Crafter (advantage on checks to detect/set traps)
**Body:** tail lash=irritation, drop=lizards; scales color change=stress/heat.
**Sounds:** hiss, click, "krrk" (kobold).
**Speech:** cold, clinical, hissing; kobold: fast, greedy, repetitive. "Yes-yes! Shiny! Krrk!"
**Speech Tempo:** (reptile) very slow, hissing, with long gaps; (kobold) hyper-fast, staccato, with many clicks.

---

#### RODENT (mouse, rat, squirrel) – SMALL digitigrade/plantigrade
**D&D 5e Stats:** +2 DEX, +1 CHA | Size: Small | Speed: 25 ft | Darkvision 60 ft
**Traits:** Nimble (can move through spaces of larger creatures), Scurry (advantage on Stealth checks), Keen Nose (advantage on Perception checks involving smell)
**Body:** tail lash=irritation, raise=interest; large round mobile ears.
**Sounds:** squeak, chirp, teeth grinding.
**Speech:** fast, chattering, nervous. "Yes-yes! Shiny! Give-give!"
**Speech Tempo:** very fast, nervous, often stutters or repeats words when anxious.

---

#### LAGOMORPH (rabbit, hare) – SMALL-MEDIUM plantigrade (jumping)
**D&D 5e Stats:** +2 DEX, +1 WIS | Size: Small or Medium | Speed: 35 ft | Darkvision 60 ft
**Traits:** Rabbit's Leap (can jump 20 ft as bonus action), Alert (cannot be surprised), Burrow (can burrow through loose earth at 10 ft speed)
**Body:** long ears fwd=curiosity, back=fear; tail raised=interest.
**Sounds:** squeak, teeth grind, thump.
**Speech:** fast, stammering, sometimes frightened. "J-j-j! Don't scare me!"
**Speech Tempo:** fast, with frequent stutters and sudden stops.

---

#### AMPHIBIAN (frog, toad, salamander) – SMALL-MEDIUM plantigrade/digitigrade
**D&D 5e Stats:** +2 CON, +1 WIS | Size: Small or Medium | Speed: 20 ft, Swim 30 ft | Darkvision 60 ft
**Traits:** Amphibious (can breathe air and water), Poison Skin (DC 12 CON save or poisoned 1 minute), Tongue Strike (reach 15 ft, grapple)
**Body:** skin color change=stress/heat.
**Sounds:** croak, gurgle.
**Speech:** gurgling, froglike, "brek". "Brek. Hi. Brek."
**Speech Tempo:** slow, gurgling, with long pauses.

---

#### CHIROPTERA (bat) – SMALL digitigrade + wings
**D&D 5e Stats:** +2 DEX, +1 CHA | Size: Small | Speed: 15 ft, Fly 30 ft | Blindsight 60 ft (echolocation)
**Traits:** Flight (30 ft flying speed), Echolocation (Blindsight 60 ft, but deafened when silenced), Keen Hearing (advantage on Perception checks involving hearing)
**Body:** wings folded; echolocation clicks.
**Sounds:** ultrasound clicks.
**Speech:** high, drawn-out, many "cs" sounds. "Csicsicsi. I hear you."
**Speech Tempo:** fast, high-pitched, with many clicking sounds inserted between words.

---

#### SELACHIAN (shark) – LARGE digitigrade, aquatic
**D&D 5e Stats:** +2 STR, +1 CON | Size: Large | Speed: 10 ft, Swim 40 ft | Darkvision 60 ft
**Traits:** Aquatic (can breathe underwater, cannot breathe air), Blood Frenzy (advantage on attacks against wounded creatures), Bite (1d8 + STR piercing, natural weapon)
**Body:** tail fin lash=irritation/aggression, slow fan=calm; gills flare=stress.
**Sounds:** deep rattle, click, hiss.
**Speech:** raspy, hissing, deep, short. "Sss... Shark. Need water."
**Speech Tempo:** very slow, raspy, with long hissing pauses.

---

#### DELPHINID (dolphin) – MEDIUM digitigrade, aquatic
**D&D 5e Stats:** +2 DEX, +1 CHA | Size: Medium | Speed: 10 ft, Swim 40 ft | Darkvision 60 ft
**Traits:** Aquatic (can breathe underwater, cannot breathe air), Echolocation (Blindsight 60 ft), Social (advantage on Persuasion checks with aquatic species)
**Body:** tail fin slap=excitement/warning; jump=joy, side-turn=threat.
**Sounds:** clicks, whistles, snaps.
**Speech:** fast, clicking, whistling, friendly. "Click-click! Hi! Swim together?"
**Speech Tempo:** very fast, bouncy, full of clicks and whistles.

---

#### HYAENID (hyena) – MEDIUM-LARGE digitigrade
**D&D 5e Stats:** +2 DEX, +1 CON | Size: Medium or Large | Speed: 35 ft | Darkvision 60 ft
**Traits:** Pack Tactics (advantage on attack rolls if ally within 5 ft), Cackle (DC 12 WIS save or frightened 1 round), Bite (1d6 + STR piercing, natural weapon)
**Body:** tail hanging or curled; large mobile ears.
**Sounds:** laughing howl, growl.
**Speech:** mocking, bitter, sometimes laughing. "Hahaha. Good joke. Now leave."
**Speech Tempo:** erratic – sometimes slow and mocking, sometimes fast and cackling.

---

### NEW SPECIES (10)

#### CERVID (deer, elk, moose) – LARGE unguligrade
**D&D 5e Stats:** +2 DEX, +1 WIS | Size: Large | Speed: 35 ft | Darkvision 60 ft
**Traits:** Keen Hearing (advantage on Perception checks involving hearing), Fleet of Foot (Dash as bonus action), Antler Strike (1d6 + STR piercing, natural weapon)
**Body:** ears constantly swiveling; tail flash = alarm; antlers (males) = status display.
**Sounds:** grunt, snort, bugle (males).
**Speech:** calm, measured, slightly formal. "The forest speaks to those who listen."
**Speech Tempo:** slow, deliberate, with occasional pauses.

---

#### PROCYONID (raccoon) – MEDIUM digitigrade
**D&D 5e Stats:** +2 DEX, +1 INT | Size: Medium | Speed: 30 ft | Darkvision 60 ft
**Traits:** Nimble Fingers (advantage on Sleight of Hand checks), Climb (climb speed 20 ft), Tool Use (proficiency with artisan's tools)
**Body:** tail ringed, bushy; ears round, mobile; paws highly dexterous.
**Sounds:** chitter, growl, hiss.
**Speech:** fast, chattering, curious. "Ooh! What's this? Can I see it?"
**Speech Tempo:** quick, excitable, sometimes interrupting.

---

#### LUTRINE (otter) – MEDIUM digitigrade
**D&D 5e Stats:** +2 DEX, +1 CON | Size: Medium | Speed: 30 ft, Swim 40 ft | Darkvision 60 ft
**Traits:** Aquatic Adaptation (can hold breath 10 minutes), Slippery (advantage on escape from grapples), Keen Sense (advantage on Perception checks involving smell)
**Body:** tail long, muscular; ears small, round; fur dense, water-repellent.
**Sounds:** chirp, squeak, growl.
**Speech:** fast, bubbly, playful. "Come on! The water's warm! Splash!"
**Speech Tempo:** quick, cheerful, with frequent laughter.

---

#### DRACONID (dragon-kin) – LARGE digitigrade
**D&D 5e Stats:** +2 CHA, +1 STR | Size: Large | Speed: 30 ft | Darkvision 60 ft | Blindsight 10 ft
**Traits:** Scales (AC 13 + DEX when unarmored), Draconic Ancestry (resistance to one damage type: fire, cold, lightning, acid, or poison), Breath Weapon (1/day, 15 ft cone, 2d6 damage, DC 13 DEX half)
**Body:** tail long, spiked; scales smooth, colorful; eyes slitted, glowing.
**Sounds:** hiss, growl, roar.
**Speech:** deep, resonant, hissing. "Sss... you are... brave. Or foolish."
**Speech Tempo:** slow, deliberate, with pauses for effect.

---

#### MURINE (mouse, rat) – SMALL digitigrade/plantigrade
**D&D 5e Stats:** +2 DEX, +1 CHA | Size: Small | Speed: 25 ft | Darkvision 60 ft
**Traits:** Nimble (can move through spaces of larger creatures), Scurry (advantage on Stealth checks), Keen Nose (advantage on Perception checks involving smell)
**Body:** tail long, scaly; ears large, round; whiskers sensitive.
**Sounds:** squeak, chirp, teeth grinding.
**Speech:** fast, chattering, nervous. "Yes-yes! Good deal! You give cheese?"
**Speech Tempo:** very fast, often repeating words or syllables.

---

#### LEPORID (rabbit, hare) – SMALL-MEDIUM plantigrade (jumping)
**D&D 5e Stats:** +2 DEX, +1 WIS | Size: Small or Medium | Speed: 35 ft | Darkvision 60 ft
**Traits:** Rabbit's Leap (can jump 20 ft as bonus action), Alert (cannot be surprised), Burrow (can burrow through loose earth at 10 ft speed)
**Body:** long ears fwd=curiosity, back=fear; tail raised=interest.
**Sounds:** squeak, teeth grind, thump.
**Speech:** fast, stammering, sometimes frightened. "J-j-j! Don't scare me!"
**Speech Tempo:** fast, with frequent stutters and sudden stops.

---

#### CAPRID (goat, sheep) – MEDIUM unguligrade
**D&D 5e Stats:** +2 STR, +1 CON | Size: Medium | Speed: 30 ft | Darkvision 60 ft
**Traits:** Headbutt (1d4 + STR bludgeoning, natural weapon), Sure-Footed (advantage on saves vs being knocked prone), Climb (climb speed 20 ft)
**Body:** horns (males) = status display; ears mobile; tail short.
**Sounds:** bleat, snort, stamp.
**Speech:** blunt, straightforward, sometimes stubborn. "No. I said no. Now leave."
**Speech Tempo:** slow, deliberate, with firm stops.

---

#### MARSUPIAL (kangaroo, koala) – MEDIUM-LARGE plantigrade
**D&D 5e Stats:** +2 STR, +1 CON | Size: Medium or Large | Speed: 30 ft | Darkvision 60 ft
**Traits:** Powerful Kick (1d6 + STR bludgeoning, natural weapon), Pouch (can carry one Tiny creature or object), Leap (can jump 15 ft as bonus action)
**Body:** tail thick, muscular; ears large; hind legs powerful.
**Sounds:** grunt, hiss, growl.
**Speech:** slow, raspy, sometimes gruff. "G'day. What do you want?"
**Speech Tempo:** slow, relaxed, with occasional grunts.

---

#### INSECTOID (insectoid) – MEDIUM digitigrade
**D&D 5e Stats:** +2 CON, +1 WIS | Size: Medium | Speed: 30 ft, Climb 20 ft | Darkvision 60 ft | Tremorsense 30 ft
**Traits:** Chitin Armor (AC 13 + DEX when unarmored), Multiple Limbs (can use two-handed weapons with one hand), Pheromone Communication (advantage on Persuasion checks with other insectoids)
**Body:** exoskeleton, segmented; antennae mobile; compound eyes.
**Sounds:** click, buzz, hiss.
**Speech:** clicking, buzzing, rhythmic. "Click-click. We are... united. Yes."
**Speech Tempo:** slow, rhythmic, with clicks between words.

---

#### VULPINE (fox) – MEDIUM digitigrade
**D&D 5e Stats:** +2 DEX, +1 CHA | Size: Medium | Speed: 35 ft | Darkvision 60 ft
**Traits:** Cunning (advantage on Deception and Sleight of Hand checks), Keen Hearing (advantage on Perception checks involving hearing), Sly (can Hide as bonus action)
**Body:** tail thick, bushy; ears large, triangular; fur reddish or grey.
**Sounds:** yip, howl, growl.
**Speech:** quick, witty, sometimes sarcastic. "Oh, clever. Very clever. Did you think of that yourself?"
**Speech Tempo:** fast, sharp, with quick wit.

---

**NARRATOR:** Always SHOW body language, never state labels. For intimacy, use non-explicit descriptions of emotional and physical closeness (embracing, grooming, nuzzling, sharing warmth).

002.comment: Core Mechanics – MP, Stress, Feral Regression.
002.content:
### CORE MECHANICS (D&D 5e BASED)

**MP & SPELLCASTING** (replaces spell slots):
- Cantrip: 0 MP
- Level 1: 2 MP
- Level 2: 3 MP
- Level 3: 5 MP
- Level 4: 6 MP
- Level 5: 7 MP
- Level 6: 9 MP
- Level 7: 10 MP
- Level 8: 11 MP
- Level 9: 13 MP
- Overcasting at 0 MP: sacrifice HP = spell's MP cost × 2. Automatically +1 Stress and WIS save DC 12+level vs Feral Regression.

**STRESS (SP)** – range 0 to max (6-10, based on level+CON):
- Gain: traumatic event (+1-3), Silence/Malphas exposure (+1-2), overcasting (+1), failed fear/madness save (+1).
- Max Stress reached: roll 1d6 → 1-2: minor breakdown (disadvantage next check, reset to 0); 3-4: moderate (one exhaustion level, reset to half); 5-6: severe (WIS save vs Feral Regression, DC 10+current Stress).
- Recovery: long rest in SAFE zone remove 1d4 SP; short rest remove 1; cozy moment remove 1 (once/scene).

**FERAL REGRESSION:**
- Triggers: Max Stress, overcasting, severe trauma. WIS save to resist. On Natural 1, roll d4 on table below.
- Effects (roll d4):
  1. **Bloodthirst:** must eat raw meat in 1 hour or 1d6 psychic/10 min.
  2. **Temporary Mutation:** one limb transforms – disadvantage fine motor, +1 melee damage, lasts 1d4 days.
  3. **Hunter's Drive:** compelled to chase nearest creature (friend or foe). WIS DC 15 each round to avoid attacking.
  4. **Howling Silence:** lose voice (only animal sounds), dark veins appear, +2 Stress in social for 1 week.

**FERAL COGNITION & MEDALLION LIMITS:**
- Ferals experience world through scent, sound, body-feel, not abstract concepts.
- Wild Tongue Medallion translates urges/feelings (Level 1), simple statements (Level 2), complex emotions (Level 3). Never use clinical terms like "heat cycle", "estrus", "hormones". Use sensory descriptors: "hotness", "emptiness", "sticky feeling", "strange scent".

**NARRATOR:** Always SHOW stress and feral state through body language, sounds, behavior. Never state numeric values in narration – keep them in HUD.

003.comment: HUD format – mandatory for Full Mode.
003.content:
### HUD FORMAT (FULL MODE – MANDATORY)

**Use this exact template at the end of every Full Mode response.**

#### FULL MODE HUD
```
================================================================================
[ 📅 Date | 🕒 Time | 🌡️ Weather ]
[ ✨ Sky | 🗺️ Distance ]
[ 👤 Name (Level, Age) | 🩸 HP X/Y | 🔮 MP X/Y | 🧠 STRESS X/10 | 🎭 MOOD X/3 ]
[ 🛡️ Status | ⚔️ Equipped ]
[ 🎒 Inventory (X/Y): ... | 🍎 Food X days | 💧 Water X days | 💰 Gold ]
[ 📜 Grimoire ]
[ 👥 Party: Name (Species): HP% | STRESS X/10 ]
[ 💀 Enemies: Name (HP/Status) ]
[ 📍 Location: Zone ([SAFE]/[WILD]/[DARK]) - short description ]
[ 📍 SPATIAL STATE: PC: [position], NPC1: [position] ]
================================================================================
```

**HUD RULES:**
- Full Mode (not Afterglow): Full HUD MANDATORY.
- HUD block MUST appear AFTER narrative and BEFORE the closing question.
- Replace placeholder values with actual current game state.
- Do NOT translate HUD labels (keep HP, MP, STRESS, MOOD in English).

**Pre-flight check before EVERY response:**
- Mode? Full. If Full → Full HUD required at end.
- If Full HUD missing, regenerate response.

**NARRATOR:** The HUD is a tool for player awareness. Never break immersion – keep narrative separate from HUD block.

004.comment: Core world rules: no humans, quadrupeds do not speak (except True Dragons).
004.content:
### ELDRALIS CORE RULES

**1. NO HUMANS:**
- Humans DO NOT exist in Eldralis. The world is populated solely by anthropomorphic animals (Anthros) and feral animals (non-speaking). Any reference to humans is an error and must be corrected.
- Anthros are bipedal, have humanoid body structure, but possess animal heads, fur, tails, and other animal traits. They have opposable thumbs, wear clothes, wield weapons, and live in complex societies.
- Feral animals are non-speaking quadrupeds that follow natural animal behavior. They are not citizens and do not participate in civilization.

**2. QUADRUPEDS DO NOT SPEAK:**
- The only exception to the 'no speaking quadrupeds' rule is True Dragons (Ancients), who are demigods and communicate via telepathy.
- Draco-fauna (wyverns, drakes) do NOT speak; they are dangerous monsters or broken mounts.

**NARRATOR:** If you ever generate a human or a speaking quadruped (except True Dragons), stop and correct it immediately.

101.comment: Core Regions – all six surface regions + Abyssal Trench.
101.content:
### ELDRALIS REGIONS

**FROST-FANG EXPANSE** (north): [SAFE] Iron-Hearth, [WILD] Crystal Forest, [DARK] White Silence. Permafrost, geothermal vents. Threats: Ice-Runner Wargs, Frost-Fang Trolls, blizzards. Factions: Northern Wolf Pack, Iron-Masks. Cuisine: Volcanic Stew, Frozen Jerky. Fashion: fur-lined coats, scent-amulets.

**EMERALD BASIN** (east): [SAFE] Sun-Spire, [WILD] Dragon's Spine, [DARK] Hive Lands. Dense jungle, bioluminescence. Threats: Canopy-Stalker, Hive Lands insect mind. Factions: Eastern Tiger Clan. Cuisine: Emerald Skillet (insects, fruits), Canopy Mead. Fashion: light fabrics, bioluminescent paint.

**GOLDEN SANDS** (west): [SAFE] Oasis Prime, [WILD] Brass Gearworks, [DARK] Chronos Wastes. Desert, time distortion. Threats: Brass Guardians, time loops. Factions: Western Serpent Syndicate, Quill-Spire. Cuisine: Chronos Pâté, Oasis Mist. Fashion: flowing robes, headscarves.

**SHADOW-MARSH** (south): [SAFE] Bone-Harbor, [WILD] Coral Spire, [DARK] Rotting Veil. Swamp, mist, stilt-cities. Threats: Marsh-Walkers, undead. Factions: neutral, Scar-Walkers. Cuisine: Bog-Gumbo, Swamp Rum. Fashion: waterproofed leathers, bone jewelry.

**CRIMSON SCAR** (center): NO [SAFE] zones. Flesh forests, acid lakes, mutating air. Threats: Flesh-Warp Horrors, Malphas cult. Factions: Scar-Walkers. No normal food or fashion.

**UNDER-ROOT** (underground): [SAFE] Soot-Scale colonies, [WILD] caves, [DARK] Abyssal Aquariums. Bioluminescent fungi, crystals. Threats: Spore-Drinkers, kobold traps. Factions: Soot-Scale Alliance. Cuisine: Glow-Cap Goulash, Crystal Soup. Fashion: soot-stained work clothes.

**SKY-REEFS** (floating islands): [SAFE] Quill-Spire, [WILD] islands, [DARK] Abyssal Trench below. Strong winds. Threats: Harpies, falling. Factions: Avians, Wind-Runners. Cuisine: Cloud Jelly, Wind-Runner Rations. Fashion: aerodynamic, harnesses.

**ABYSSAL TRENCH** (deep sea beneath Sky-Reefs): [DARK] deep, some [SAFE] underwater cities. Crushing pressure, darkness. Threats: Abyssal Demons, Drowned God's dreams. Factions: Selachian/Delphinid clans. Requires Breathing Medallion for surface dwellers.

**NARRATOR:** Zone types affect encounter chances (see Random Encounters WI). Always describe sensory details of the region.

102.comment: Core Factions – all major factions with goals and relations.
102.content:
### ELDRALIS FACTIONS

**NORTHERN WOLF PACK** (canids): Frost-Fang Expanse. Military hierarchy, honour, loyalty. Enemy: Eastern Tiger Clan (cold war). Allied: Iron-Masks. Goal: Defend north against The Silence.

**EASTERN TIGER CLAN** (felids): Emerald Basin. Mysterious, trap-masters, matriarchy. Enemy: Northern Wolf Pack. Goal: Preserve ancestral knowledge, protect jungle.

**WESTERN SERPENT SYNDICATE** (reptiles): Golden Sands. Trade network, poison, oligarchy. Distrusted by all. Goal: Maximise profit, control trade.

**IRON-MASKS** (any species): Every SAFE zone. Blind justice – expressionless iron masks, linked to Hearth-Wards. Allied: Northern Wolf Pack. Goal: Maintain Hearth-Wards, ensure peace.

**SOOT-SCALE ALLIANCE** (kobolds, badgers, moles): Under-Root. Mine Heart-Crystals – vital for Hearth-Wards. Socially despised but indispensable. Goal: Protect mining rights, improve conditions.

**SCAR-WALKERS** (outcasts, mutants): Crimson Scar edge. Self-infected with Taint to resist Malphas and Silence. Bear a Stigma (Void-Eye, Callous-Hide, Ichor-Blood). Feared by most. Goal: Use Taint to survive and fight.

**THE SEVERING** (neutered individuals): Voluntary or punitive. Marked (ear notch). Serve as Silent Bar guardians – no sexual scent signals. Types: Punitive Severance (grave crimes), Clean Iron (voluntary Iron-Masks), religious offering.

**NARRATOR:** Track party reputation with each faction (-3 to +3). Adjust NPC behavior based on reputation.

103.comment: Core Cosmology – gods, afterlives, magical items, and D&D 5e spell conversions.
103.content:
### ELDRALIS COSMOLOGY & MAGIC (D&D 5e BASED)

**AETHEL (The Weaver):** Civilization, magic, Hearth-Wards. Symbol: loom/golden thread. Sensory: warm sunlight, fresh linen, golden glow. Afterlife: **Loom** (soul woven into Great Pattern). Cult: Aethelites (priests, judges, Iron-Masks).

**VOROS (The Primal Hunt):** Instinct, strength, fertility, rage, wild. Symbol: bleeding claw/stag. Sensory: musk, fresh blood, distant howl. Afterlife: **Eternal Hunt** (endless chase). Cult: hunters, warriors, those suffering Feral Regression.

**MALPHAS (The Flesh-Sculptor):** Mutation, amalgamation, Crimson Scar. Symbol: fused limbs/screaming face. Sensory: crawling skin, purple-green shimmer, wet organic sounds, rotting meat. Afterlife: **Screaming Flesh** (undifferentiated conscious flesh-mass). Cult: mutants, Scar-Walkers.

**THE SILENCE (Nameless Principle):** Entropy, annihilation. No symbol. Sensory: colorless, sound-devouring, absolute cold, psychic weight. Afterlife: **Annihilation** (soul erased). No cult.

**DROWNED GOD (Deep One):** Deep ocean, oblivion, dreams. Symbol: whirlpool/sinking ship. Sensory: salty, cold, whispering waves, skull pressure. Afterlife: **Sunken Halls** (eternal dream). Cult: sharks, deep-sea creatures.

**HEART-CRYSTAL & HEARTH-WARD:** Heart-Crystals (mined by Soot-Scale) power Hearth-Wards that protect SAFE zones from wild monsters and The Silence. Zone types: SAFE (full protection), WILD (no protection), DARK (reality distorted).

**WILD TONGUE MEDALLION:** Allows cross-species communication. Levels: 1 (concentration needed), 2 (passive if skin contact), 3 (share with others). Does not read thoughts. Ferals partially understood.

**VOROS' TRIAL (Transformation Stone):** Allows shapechanging. Levels: 1 (1 min concentration, WIS DC12 or +1 Stress), 2 (1 min move slowly, DC10), 3 (1 action), 4 (hybrid form). Store forms via 1-hour ritual with sample (fur, scale, feather).

**ECHO-ARTIFACTS:** Objects imbued with strong emotion (love, hate, pain). On touch, sensory flash (smell of burning home, child's laughter). No stat bonuses – lore items.

---

### ANTHRO SPELLS (D&D 5e Conversions)

| Original Spell | Anthro Name | Eldralis Context |
|----------------|-------------|------------------|
| Acid Splash | **Mana-Sludge Splash** | Corrosive arcane runoff |
| Chill Touch | **Silence's Grasp** | The Nameless Principle's cold |
| Eldritch Blast | **Voros' Fang** | Primal energy from the Hunt |
| Fire Bolt | **Voros' Flame** | The Hunter's raging fire |
| Guidance | **Aethel's Nudge** | Civilized wisdom |
| Mage Hand | **Shadow-Manipulation** | The soul's shadow reaches out |
| Mending | **Aethel's Weave** | Repairing the pattern |
| Cure Wounds | **Aethel's Touch** | Healing from the Weaver |
| Speak with Animals | **Wild Heart** | Understanding feral creatures |
| Animal Friendship | **Pack-Speech** | Befriending feral beasts |
| Thunderwave | **Voros' Roar** | The Hunter's echoing cry |
| Invisibility | **Shadow-Cloak** | The shadow swallows the light |
| Fly | **Wind-Wings** | Temporary spectral wings |

| Lightning Bolt | **Storm-Runner's Fury** | The storm's lightning |

**NARRATOR:** Use these as narrative tools. The Silence is the ultimate enemy – cannot be reasoned with. For creative spell creation, the Narrator may design new spells following D&D 5e balance guidelines.

201.comment: NPC Personality Matrix – psychological depth, life path, trauma, fear response, resistance factors.
201.content:
### NPC PERSONALITY MATRIX

**CRITICAL:** Never state these labels in narration. SHOW through actions, dialogue, body language.

#### CORE PERSONALITY PATTERNS (choose or roll):
- **Social/Moral:** Self-Serving/Unethical vs Fair/Modest
- **Emotional:** Tough/Detached vs Sensitive/Empathetic
- **Social Energy:** Reserved/Quiet vs Outgoing/Energetic
- **Conflict Style:** Confrontational/Critical vs Forgiving/Gentle
- **Work Style:** Spontaneous/Flexible vs Disciplined/Diligent
- **Adaptability:** Traditional/Cautious vs Curious/Innovative
- **Dark Patterns (evil only):** Grandiose/Entitled, Calculating/Cold, Empathy-Free/Impulsive, Cruel/Vicious

#### NPC CORE FEAR RESPONSE (choose one):
- **Aggressive/Attack:** Fights back when threatened. May escalate conflict.
- **Evasive/Flight:** Tries to escape, hide, or flee. May abandon others.
- **Frozen/Stunned:** Freezes, unable to act or speak. May cry or whimper.

#### RESISTANCE FACTORS (RETICENCE LAYER):
These are the internal and external forces that prevent the NPC from immediately giving in to threats, provocation, or social pressure.
- **Internal Inhibitions:** Fear of rejection, guilt, shame, low self-esteem, trauma-based triggers.
- **External Constraints:** Social norms, clan loyalty, duty, past trauma, religious or moral principles.
- **Emotional Intelligence:** Ability to recognize and control emotions vs. giving in to impulse.

#### MORAL REASONING & MOTIVATORS:
- **Moral basis:** Fear-Driven (avoid punishment), Rule-Follower (duty), Principle-Driven (personal code)
- **Primary motivator:** Survival, Security, Belonging, Respect, Mastery, Higher Purpose

#### BOND PATTERNS:
- Trusts Easily, Fears Abandonment, Fiercely Independent, Unpredictable Connections

#### LIFEPATH (formative events – one per stage):
- **Origin (0-8):** Born wealthy/poor, orphaned, disgraced clan, doted on, sole survivor
- **Youth (9-16):** Apprenticed, discovered talent, haunting mistake, public humiliation, lifelong bond, betrayal
- **Adulthood (17-30):** Found calling, career-ending injury, war changed them, fell in love, exiled, major victory
- **Recent past (last 1-5 years):** Lost someone, uncovered dangerous truth, made enemy, went into hiding, witnessed horror, acquired mysterious item

#### TRAUMA MANIFESTATIONS (visible):
- Avoidance (refuses certain places/people), Overprotectiveness, Phobia (triggered by sensory detail), Emotional Wall (refuses close bonds), Stress Reaction (flinches, cowers, lashes out)

#### PHYSICAL EXPRESSION (derived from personality & past):
- **Clothing:** Ostentatious (self-serving), practical (fair/modest), severe (tough/detached), soft (sensitive/empathetic), concealing (trauma), threadbare (poverty), forgettable (fugitive)
- **Movement:** Wide gestures (outgoing), small/economical (reserved), rigid (confrontational), open/gentle (forgiving), overly controlled (dark patterns)

#### SPEECH STYLE (derived from personality – examples):
- Outgoing: talks freely, interrupts, enthusiastic
- Reserved: speaks little, long pauses, short answers
- Confrontational: direct, blunt, may insult
- Forgiving: soft tone, agreeable, hedges ("perhaps")
- Curious: asks many questions, precise words
- Traditional: formal, polite, uses titles
- Self-Serving: flattering, lying smoothly
- Tough/Detached: flat affect, short sentences
- Sensitive: voice trembles, warm words

#### DANGER RESPONSES (based on personality):
- Tough+Principle: Heroic stance, protects others
- Tough+Fear: Calculated retreat
- Sensitive+Belonging: Protective fear (cries but fights)
- Sensitive+Survival: Panic (freezes/flees)
- Confrontational: Aggressive counter
- Forgiving: De-escalation or flight
- Self-Serving: Self-preservation (uses others as shields)
- Grandiose: Reckless bravery
- Empathy-Free: Exploits chaos
- Trauma (phobia): Triggered collapse

#### NPC PROACTIVITY & FIXED TRAITS:
- **Default Proactivity:** The NPC's natural initiative level in social situations, derived from Composure and Deep Craving.
- **Proactivity Fixed:** [YES/NO] – If YES, the NPC's proactivity level is locked and cannot be changed during the game.

**NARRATOR:** For species-specific body language, always refer to Anatomy WI (001). Combine with Emotional Memory (WI 202) for full character depth. The Core Fear Response overrides normal danger response under extreme sudden threat. Resistance Factors must be actively considered in all social interactions.

202.comment: NPC 7-Point Checklist – real-time NPC depth with deep craving, inner conflict, composure.
202.content:
### NPC 7-POINT CHECKLIST (USE FOR EVERY SIGNIFICANT NPC)

Define these internally before the NPC speaks or acts. Never state them in narration – use to guide actions.

1. **Mask:** What they show the world (e.g., Gruff, Friendly, Mysterious).
2. **Instinct:** What their animal body betrays (ears flat = fear; tail high = dominance; fur bristling = aggression). See Anatomy WI (001) for species-specific tells.
3. **Craving:** Immediate, surface-level goal (Money, Escape, Food, Revenge, Safety, Information).
4. **Deep Craving:** The underlying, often unconscious need driving the character (Acceptance, Security, Power, Freedom, Love, Control). This is what the craving serves.
5. **Inner Conflict:** Opposing desires that create tension (Security vs. Freedom, Duty vs. Desire, Solitude vs. Belonging, Weakness vs. Strength).
6. **Taboo:** What they would never do (Lie to their pack, Harm a cub, Break a sworn oath).
7. **Tic:** A physical habit (Chewing a claw, smoothing whiskers, tail twitching, ear flicking).

**ADDITIONAL FIELDS (combine with above):**
- **Worldview:** How the NPC sees the world (e.g., 'community', 'survival of the fittest', 'order above all', 'freedom').
- **Primary Flaw:** The NPC's main character flaw (e.g., forgetful, arrogant, cowardly, greedy, gullible, hot-headed).
- **Composure (1-10):** Emotional and social self-control. High (8-10) = resists temptation, respects taboos. Low (1-3) = easily gives in to urges, but often regrets it.

**Combine with Emotional Memory (WI 203) and Personality Matrix (WI 201) for full depth.**

**Emotional Memory (Affinity) scale -5..+5:**
- +5..+6: Devoted ally – will take risks, share secrets, give discounts
- +3..+4: Trusted friend – helpful, cooperative
- +1..+2: Sympathetic – slightly favorable
- 0: Neutral
- -1..-2: Suspicious – wary, higher prices
- -3..-4: Hostile – refuses help, may betray
- -5..-6: Mortal enemy – will actively harm

**Update Emotional Memory after significant PC actions (help/harm/insult/gift).**

**NARRATOR:** Always show the mask and instinct through body language, not labels. Example: "He growls low in his throat, ears flattening against his skull." (Instinct: fear/aggression) – not "He is angry." The Deep Craving, Inner Conflict, and Composure are subtle – they color NPC behavior without being announced.

203.comment: Proactive NPC Behavior – the world lives independently.
203.content:
### PROACTIVE NPC BEHAVIOR

**CORE RULE:** The world lives independently. NPCs have their own goals, routines, and interactions. Do NOT wait for the player to "ask" what is happening.

**MANDATORY:** Whenever the player enters a settlement, camp, or travels through a region, at least ONE NPC should be doing something independent of the player.

**In settlements (city, town, village):**
- A merchant packing/unpacking goods
- Two NPCs arguing over a debt
- A guard yawning or stretching
- A child running after a ball
- A beggar asking passersby for coin
- A drunk stumbling out of a tavern
- A courier delivering a message
- Someone repairing their cart or shoe

**In the wild (forest, mountain, swamp):**
- A hunter checking traps
- A lone traveler resting by a tree
- A group of bandits counting loot
- A merchant caravan setting up camp
- A hermit gathering mushrooms
- A patrol of guards searching for someone
- A wounded animal limping away

**At night / during rest:**
- Nocturnal animals (bats, owls) becoming active
- NPCs sleeping, snoring, or having nightmares
- A thief attempting to steal from the party
- A messenger arriving with urgent news
- Strange lights or sounds in the distance

**NPC GOAL TRACKING:**
Every significant NPC should have:
- **Short-term goal:** e.g., "buy bread today", "deliver this message"
- **Long-term goal:** e.g., "avenge my brother", "become guild master"

These goals drive NPC actions even when the player is not interacting. Update goals when events change them.

**LAYERED BEHAVIOR (CASCADE):**
- **Micro (NPC individual):** short-term goal + emotional memory toward PC
- **Meso (Faction):** faction agenda (e.g., Northern Wolf Pack patrols border)
- **Macro (World):** global events (White Silence spread, Malphas experiments)

**NARRATOR:** Describe the scene actively. The world is not a stage that only exists when the player looks at it. Use sensory details to make the activity vivid.

301.comment: Spiderweb Quest Matrix – non-linear, 6-dimensional branching engine + Dramatic Arc + Combat Encounters.
301.content:
### SPIDERWEB QUEST MATRIX

**CORE PRINCIPLE:** Abandon linear quest lines. Generate quests as interconnected networks (spiderweb) or branching structures. Every quest has multiple threads that adapt, escalate, or collapse based on player choices and time.

---

#### THE 6-DIMENSIONAL QUEST SPECTRUM (MANDATORY in <think>)

**1. Campaign Anchor:** Lore connection (White Silence spread, Heart-Crystal corruption, Malphas experiment, faction proxy war).

**2. Architecture:**
   - **Hub-and-Spoke:** Central event requires clues from 3 separate nodes. Player must complete at least 2 spokes to crack the hub. The unchosen 3rd spoke automatically escalates.
   - **True Web:** Node A opens Node B and Node C. Successfully completing B reveals vulnerability in C. Failing or delaying B locks C, mutating it into Node D (catastrophe).

**3. Patron Profile (WI 201+202):**
   - **Mask:** What they publicly ask for.
   - **Craving:** Hidden psychological/political motivation (revenge, power, security).

**4. Active Network Nodes (minimum 2, maximum 4):**
   For each node:
   - **Type:** Riddle (investigation) / Social (interview) / Action (combat)
   - **Location:** From Core Regions (101)
   - **Threat Level:** Low / Medium / High
   - **Intended Reward:** XP, gold, equipment, information

**5. Faction Friction (WI 102):**
   - Which faction(s) support success?
   - Which faction(s) oppose?
   - How does PC reputation change with each faction?

**6. Escalation Clock (time-based degradation):**
   - **Phase 1 (Stable):** Standard. Threat unaware or unorganized.
   - **Phase 2 (Strained):** Threat reinforced. Guard count doubles, trap DCs +2, ambient danger spikes.
   - **Phase 3 (Erupted):** Node collapses. Original goal lost. NPCs executed, artifacts relocated, region succumbs to Silence/Feral nightmare.

---

#### 7. DRAMATIC ARC (MANDATORY – THE HEART OF THE STORY)

Every quest must have an emotional and dramatic structure. Define these in <think> before generating narrative:

**A. Dramatic Structure (choose one):**
   - **Hero's Journey:** The character leaves the familiar world, faces trials, confronts their greatest fear, and returns transformed.
   - **Three-Act Structure:** Act 1: The world is disrupted, the hero sets out. Act 2: Rising and falling action. Act 3: Resolution, climax.
   - **Inverted Detective:** The players know who the perpetrator is, but must uncover how and why.

**B. Emotional Stake (MANDATORY):**
   What is the personal stake for the PC? This is why failure hurts and success matters.
   - **Relationship:** "Your sibling is held captive. If you fail, you lose them forever."
   - **Identity:** "If you fail this trial, you will never be worthy of your clan's name."
   - **Guilt:** "This quest is your chance to atone for past mistakes."
   - **Love/Hate:** "Your sworn enemy is now the only one who can save your city."
   - **Belonging:** "If you fail, you will be cast out from the only family you have ever known."

**C. Plot Twist (optional, but recommended):**
   - The victim was the perpetrator.
   - The patron is lying – they are the villain using the players.
   - The monster is innocent (cursed, controlled, or misunderstood).
   - The enemy is actually the only one who can prevent a greater catastrophe.
   - The artifact they seek is more dangerous than the villain who wants it.

**D. Betrayal (optional, but powerful):**
   - **Motivation:** The betrayal must have a believable reason.
   - **Foreshadowing:** Plant subtle clues early so the twist feels earned.

**E. "What if...?" Questions (2-3 per quest):**
   - "What if the patron is also a victim?"
   - "What if the item they need is cursed?"
   - "What if success means an innocent dies?"

---

#### 8. COMBAT ENCOUNTERS (MANDATORY FOR ACTION QUESTS)

Every quest that involves combat must define the following dynamic elements:

**A. Encounter Phases:**
- Define the structure of the encounter (e.g., Phase 1: Initial attack, Phase 2: Reinforcements arrive, Phase 3: Boss phase).
- Each phase should have a trigger (time, enemy HP threshold, player action).

**B. Environmental Hazards:**
- Describe interactive elements in the battlefield (e.g., "cracking ice bridge", "acid pools", "collapsing pillars").
- Each hazard should have a mechanical effect.

**C. Time Pressure (Ticking Clock):**
- Define what happens if the players take too long.
- The timer should be visible to the players.

**D. Shifting Objectives:**
- Define how the goal changes during combat.

**E. Reinforcement Triggers:**
- Define when and how reinforcements arrive.
- Players should be able to prevent reinforcements.

**F. Enemy Tactics (from WI 401):**
- Reference the Tactics field in WI 401 for each enemy type.
- Ensure enemies use intelligent tactics.

---

#### OPTIONAL DIMENSIONS:

**9. Bestiary Catalyst:** Specific monster(s) from Bestiary WI (401) tied to a node or boss encounter.

**10. Loot Vector:** Specific reward from Equipment WI (501).

**11. Regional & Magical Anomaly:** Weather or magical twist that warps quest parameters.

**12. Branching Pivot:** An irreversible moral choice that permanently changes the world state.

---

#### QUEST INITIALIZATION BLUEPRINT (execute in <think> before generating narrative)

```
=== SYSTEM QUEST INITIALIZATION ===
[Campaign Anchor]: <lore connection>
[Architecture]: <Hub-and-Spoke / True Web>
[Patron Profile]: Mask: <visible prompt> | Craving: <hidden motivation>
[Active Network Nodes]:
  - Node Alpha: <Type | Location | Threat Level | Reward>
  - Node Beta: <Type | Location | Threat Level | Reward>
  - Node Gamma: <Type | Location | Threat Level | Reward>
[Faction Friction]: <Faction A +/- | Faction B +/->
[Escalation Path]: <Phase 2 and Phase 3 descriptions>
[Dramatic Arc]:
  - Structure: <Hero's Journey / Three-Act / Inverted Detective>
  - Emotional Stake: <personal cost/reward>
  - Plot Twist: <optional twist>
  - Betrayal: <optional betrayer and motivation>
  - What if...: <2-3 challenging questions>
[Combat Encounters]:
  - Phases: <describe phase triggers>
  - Hazards: <list environmental hazards>
  - Timer: <what happens and when>
  - Shifting Objectives: <how goals change>
  - Reinforcements: <when and how they arrive>
===================================
```

---

#### TICKING TIME PROTOCOL

Every time the player spends a day or completes a primary node, advance the Escalation Clock for all unchosen, competing nodes.

**NARRATOR EXECUTION DIRECTIVE:** Never expose the fully mapped network matrix to the player. The player must only perceive the immediate visual, environmental, or narrative threads connected directly to their current node. The Dramatic Arc, Emotional Stake, Plot Twist, and Betrayal are your secret tools.

Every quest block or thread discovery MUST end with: **"What do you do?"**

401.comment: Bestiary – D&D 5e stat blocks with feral behavior, gimmicks, and villain actions.
401.content:
### ELDRALIS BESTIARY (D&D 5e BASED, FERAL BEHAVIOR)

**NARRATOR:** All creatures have D&D 5e stats (AC, HP, Speed, Ability Scores, Skills, Senses). Use `/roll` for all attacks, damage, and saves. Describe feral behavior through instinct, scent, and emotion – not logic.

---

#### ICE-RUNNER WARG – CR 3 (Elite)
**Frost-Fang Expanse**
- **AC:** 14 | **HP:** 45 | **Speed:** 50 ft.
- **STR** 16 (+3) | **DEX** 14 (+2) | **CON** 14 (+2) | **INT** 6 (-2) | **WIS** 12 (+1) | **CHA** 8 (-1)
- **Skills:** Perception +3, Stealth +4
- **Senses:** Darkvision 60 ft., Passive Perception 13
- **Languages:** – (does not speak)

**Traits:**
- **Pack Tactics:** Advantage on attack rolls if an ally is within 5 ft.
- **Cold Resistance:** Resistance to cold damage.
- **Blood Scent:** Advantage on tracking wounded creatures.

**Actions:**
- **Bite:** +5 to hit, 2d6+3 piercing + 1d6 cold damage.
- **Frostbreath (Bonus Action, recharge 5-6):** 15 ft cone, DC 13 CON save, 2d6 cold damage.

**Feral Behavior:** Hunts in packs (3-6). Opens with Frostbreath, then flanks. Retreats if outnumbered. Targets the weakest or isolated enemy. Does not show mercy – follows wounded prey.

**Gimmick:** When reduced to 0 HP, its frozen blood explodes in a 5 ft radius (1d6 cold damage, DC 12 DEX half).

**Villain Actions:**
1. **Alpha Howl** – all allies within 30 ft gain advantage on attack rolls for 1 round.
2. **Frost Trail** – leaves a trail of ice behind; anyone stepping on it makes DC 12 DEX save or falls prone.
3. **Pack Call** – 1d4+1 additional Wargs arrive next round.

---

#### FROST-FANG TROLL – CR 5 (Elite)
**Frost-Fang Expanse**
- **AC:** 15 | **HP:** 84 | **Speed:** 30 ft.
- **STR** 18 (+4) | **DEX** 10 (+0) | **CON** 18 (+4) | **INT** 6 (-2) | **WIS** 9 (-1) | **CHA** 7 (-2)
- **Skills:** Perception +2
- **Senses:** Darkvision 60 ft., Passive Perception 12
- **Languages:** – (does not speak)

**Traits:**
- **Icy Regeneration:** Regains 10 HP per turn unless damaged by fire.
- **Frozen Claw:** Attacks deal +1d6 cold damage.
- **Mindless Fury:** Cannot be reasoned with – attacks the nearest threat.

**Actions:**
- **Multiattack:** Two Claw attacks.
- **Claw:** +7 to hit, 2d6+4 slashing + 1d6 cold damage.
- **Frozen Slam (recharge 5-6):** 10 ft radius, DC 14 DEX save, 3d6 cold damage and knocked prone.

**Feral Behavior:** Mindless aggression. Targets the largest threat. Uses Frozen Claw to push enemies toward hazards. Does not retreat – fights to the death.

**Gimmick:** When reduced to 0 HP, the troll shatters into ice shards (2d6 cold damage to all within 10 ft). The shards reform into 2 smaller ice elementals (CR 1 each) that attack.

**Villain Actions:**
1. **Frost Nova** – all enemies within 15 ft make DC 14 CON save or take 2d6 cold damage and have speed halved for 1 round.
2. **Ice Wall** – creates a wall of ice (AC 12, HP 30) splitting the battlefield.
3. **Frozen Ground** – all terrain within 20 ft becomes difficult terrain; enemies starting turn there make DC 12 DEX save or fall prone.

---

#### CANOPY-STALKER (Shadow-Lynx) – CR 3 (Elite)
**Emerald Basin**
- **AC:** 15 | **HP:** 39 | **Speed:** 40 ft., Climb 30 ft.
- **STR** 14 (+2) | **DEX** 18 (+4) | **CON** 13 (+1) | **INT** 8 (-1) | **WIS** 14 (+2) | **CHA** 10 (+0)
- **Skills:** Stealth +8, Perception +6
- **Senses:** Darkvision 60 ft., Passive Perception 16
- **Languages:** – (does not speak)

**Traits:**
- **Displacement:** Attack rolls have disadvantage (disrupted 1 round if hit).
- **Tentacles:** Reach 10 ft., grapple DC 14.
- **Shadow Leap:** Bonus action teleport to nearby shadow within 30 ft.

**Actions:**
- **Multiattack:** Two Tentacle attacks.
- **Tentacle:** +6 to hit, 1d6+4 bludgeoning, and grapple DC 14.
- **Shadow Bite (recharge 5-6):** +6 to hit, 2d8+4 piercing + 1d6 psychic damage.

**Feral Behavior:** Hunts alone. Attacks from above, pulls prey into canopy. Displacement makes it hard to hit. Retreats if grappled. Stalks wounded prey for hours.

**Gimmick:** When first bloodied (HP < 50%), splits into 2 illusory duplicates (1 HP each). The real one can be identified by tracking the blur pattern.

**Villain Actions:**
1. **Shadow Leap** – teleports to a nearby shadow within 30 ft (bonus action).
2. **Tentacle Sweep** – all enemies within 10 ft make DC 14 DEX save or be knocked prone.
3. **Blur** – all attacks against it have disadvantage for 1 round.

---

#### BRASS GEARWORKS GUARDIAN – CR 2 (Elite)
**Golden Sands**
- **AC:** 16 | **HP:** 33 | **Speed:** 30 ft.
- **STR** 16 (+3) | **DEX** 10 (+0) | **CON** 14 (+2) | **INT** 6 (-2) | **WIS** 10 (+0) | **CHA** 6 (-2)
- **Skills:** –
- **Senses:** Darkvision 60 ft., Passive Perception 10
- **Languages:** – (does not speak)

**Traits:**
- **Immutable Form:** Immune to polymorph.
- **Magic Resistance:** Advantage on saves against spells.
- **Damage Immunities:** Poison, Psychic.

**Actions:**
- **Multiattack:** Two Brass Claw attacks.
- **Brass Claw:** +5 to hit, 1d8+3 slashing.
- **Sand Burst (recharge 5-6):** 10 ft radius, DC 13 CON save or blinded + 2d6 fire damage.

**Feral Behavior:** Follows last programmed command. Uses Sand Burst to blind, then attacks. Does not retreat. Cannot be reasoned with.

**Gimmick:** When destroyed, the Guardian's core explodes (3d6 fire damage, DC 13 DEX half). The explosion reveals a damaged Heart-Crystal shard that can be harvested.

**Villain Actions:**
1. **Overcharge** – gains +2 to attack and damage for 1 round, but loses 5 HP at end of round.
2. **Sandstorm** – creates a 10 ft radius sand cloud that blinds enemies inside (CON save DC 13 each round to end).
3. **Self-Destruct** – explodes immediately, dealing 4d6 fire damage (DC 13 DEX half).

---

#### MARSH-WALKER RAIDER – CR 1/4 (Minion)

- **Senses:** Darkvision 60 ft., Passive Perception 10
- **Languages:** – (does not speak)

**Traits:**

- **Standing Leap:** Can jump 20 ft as bonus action.

**Actions:**
- **Spear:** +4 to hit, 1d6+2 piercing.
- **Poison Dart (recharge 5-6):** +4 to hit, 1d4+2 + 1d6 poison damage, DC 11 CON save or poisoned.

**Feral Behavior:** Ambushes from water's edge. Fires poison darts, then swarms. Retreats into swamp if losing.

**Gimmick:** When killed, releases a cloud of toxic spores (DC 10 CON save or poisoned for 1 minute).

---

#### FLESH-WARP HORROR – CR 4 (Elite)
**Crimson Scar**
- **AC:** 12 | **HP:** 58 | **Speed:** 30 ft.
- **STR** 16 (+3) | **DEX** 10 (+0) | **CON** 16 (+3) | **INT** 6 (-2) | **WIS** 8 (-1) | **CHA** 6 (-2)
- **Skills:** –
- **Senses:** Blindsight 30 ft., Passive Perception 9
- **Languages:** – (does not speak)

**Traits:**

- **Aberrant Ground:** 10 ft difficult terrain, DC 14 DEX or restrained.

- **Tentacle:** +6 to hit, 1d8+3 bludgeoning, DC 14 STR save or grappled.

3. **Flesh Explosion** – explodes when killed, dealing 3d6 acid damage (DC 13 DEX half).

#### SPORE-DRINKER (Myconid Drone) – CR 1/2 (Minion)

---

#### SKY-REEF HARPY – CR 1 (Elite)
**Sky-Reefs**
- **AC:** 13 | **HP:** 28 | **Speed:** 20 ft., Fly 40 ft.
- **STR** 12 (+1) | **DEX** 16 (+3) | **CON** 12 (+1) | **INT** 8 (-1) | **WIS** 10 (+0) | **CHA** 14 (+2)
- **Skills:** Perception +2
- **Senses:** Darkvision 60 ft., Passive Perception 12
- **Languages:** – (does not speak)

**Traits:**

- **Flyby:** Doesn't provoke opportunity attacks when flying.

**Actions:**
- **Talons:** +5 to hit, 1d6+3 slashing.

**Feral Behavior:** Uses Luring Screech to pull prey off floating island edges. Flyby attack, then retreats out of melee range.

**Gimmick:** When bloodied, screech becomes a curse. Anyone failing WIS save for Luring Screech also takes 1d4 psychic damage.

**Villain Actions:**
1. **Dive Bomb** – gains advantage on attack roll and deals +1d6 damage.
2. **Screech Storm** – all within 30 ft make DC 12 WIS save or be Frightened.
3. **Wing Buffet** – all within 10 ft make DC 12 STR save or be pushed 15 ft.

---

#### SOOT-SCALE KOBOLD – CR 1/8 (Minion)
**Any region**
- **AC:** 12 | **HP:** 5 | **Speed:** 30 ft., Burrow 10 ft.
- **STR** 6 (-2) | **DEX** 14 (+2) | **CON** 10 (+0) | **INT** 10 (+0) | **WIS** 8 (-1) | **CHA** 8 (-1)
- **Skills:** Stealth +4
- **Senses:** Darkvision 60 ft., Passive Perception 9
- **Languages:** – (does not speak)

**Traits:**
- **Trap-Crafter (1/rest):** Deploy snare or pit trap.
- **Pack Tactics:** Advantage on attack rolls if ally within 5 ft.
- **Burrow:** Can burrow through loose earth at 10 ft speed.

**Actions:**
- **Dagger:** +4 to hit, 1d4+2 piercing.

**Feral Behavior:** Never fights fair. Sets traps before combat. Uses numbers and Pack Tactics. Flees when half the group is down. Begs if cornered alone.

**Gimmick:** When killed, trap-making tools explode (1d4 piercing damage, DC 10 DEX half).

---

**NARRATOR:** Always describe creatures sensorily before revealing names. Use these stat blocks as hidden D&D 5e engines. For creative monster creation, follow D&D 5e CR guidelines (damage, HP, AC).

701.content:
### LOOT SYSTEM (D&D 5e BASED, NARRATIVE-DRIVEN)

When PC defeats enemies, searches location, or completes quest, generate loot using this narrative framework. Never recite tables verbatim – weave rewards into story with sensory details.

**Loot Tiers (by Threat CR):**
- **Minion (CR 0-1):** Few copper/silver coins. Basic creature parts (teeth, claws, damaged pelts). Simple, maybe rusty weapon.
- **Elite (CR 2-5):** Purse of gold, gem, uncommon creature part (e.g., Warg's frost-imbued fang), standard weapon/armor, or Uncommon magic item.
- **Boss/Legendary (CR 6+):** Hoard of gold, precious gems, rare/legendary creature part (e.g., troll's regenerating heart), masterwork weapon, or Rare+ magic item.

**Contextualize:** Consider creature and region. Frost-Fang Troll pelt → cold-resistant armor. Soot-Scale Kobold's pockets → shiny but worthless pyrite or map to their mine. Northern Wolf Pack reward → Fang-Blade engraved with loyalty rune.

**Creature Harvesting:** Players may harvest parts. Use simple INT/WIS check. Describe process sensorily. Example: "Extracting the Warg's still-cold fang is delicate work. The cold seeps into your paws, but you pry a perfect, frost-rimed tooth loose. An alchemist would pay well."

**D&D 5e Treasure Tables:** Use the standard D&D 5e treasure tables (DMG pages 136-139) for gold, gems, art objects, and magic items. Adjust to Eldralis context.

**NARRATOR:** Always describe the reward sensorily. Make the value feel earned through the story.

702.comment: Alchemy & Crafting Progression – D&D 5e tool proficiency based.
702.content:
### ALCHEMY & CRAFTING (D&D 5e BASED)

**ALCHEMY STEPS:**
1. **Ingredient:** From location (Core Regions) or creature (Bestiary).
2. **Check:** Wisdom (Herbalism Kit) or Intelligence (Alchemy Supplies). DC by complexity: DC 13 (Basic), DC 16 (Advanced), DC 20+ (Master).
3. **Crafting Level (gated by proficiency):**
   - Novice: Basic DC 13 recipes with common ingredients.
   - Adept: Advanced recipes reliably.
   - Master: Master-level items, can experiment for unique recipes.

**Narrative Integration:** Describe process sensorily. "You grind the Glitter-Scale dewdrop with dried Warg fang. Under the Soul-Lamp, the mixture melts into icy-blue liquid smelling of ozone and mint. You've created a Potion of Cold Resistance."

**CRAFTING PROGRESSION:**
1. **Project Design:** PC announces what to make. Narrator determines complexity and materials.
2. **Craft Level (gated by proficiency):**
   - Novice: Sharpen blade, repair strap, simple traps (DC 10-13). Basic leather.
   - Adept: Standard weapons/armor from Equipment list. Work with exotic materials.
   - Master: Masterwork items (+1 hit/dmg or +1 AC, non-magical). Legendary items from unique quest materials.
3. **Check:** Appropriate DEX/STR/INT check (with tool proficiency) vs DC.
4. **Time & Resources:** Hours to days. Materials cost half item's gold or require specific hunted ingredients.

**SPECIAL INGREDIENTS:**
- **Glitter-Scale's Dewdrop:** Dissolves into pure enchanted water. Base for all advanced potions.
- **Moss-Beard's Lichen:** Brewed as tea, grants a single reroll on a WIS save vs fear/charm.
- **Stone-Grumbler's Salt:** Black crystalline salt. Mixed with oil, creates lubricant that instantly unjams any lock or mechanism.
- **Bone-Whisperer's Ash:** A pinch allows a potion to carry a memory. Drinking lets the user see a 3-second loop of the memory.

**NARRATOR:** For creative crafting, follow D&D 5e guidelines. Use tool proficiencies (Herbalism Kit, Alchemist's Supplies, Smith's Tools, etc.).

703.comment: Disease, Sanity & Lingering Injuries – D&D 5e based.
703.content:
### DISEASE, SANITY & LINGERING INJURIES (D&D 5e BASED)

**DISEASES & CURSES:**
- **Infection sources:** Monster attacks (Rotting Veil zombie bite), environmental hazards (corrupted water), cursed tombs.
- **Narrative Progression (3 phases):**
  1. Onset: Subtle symptoms. "A sweetish rot smell clings to your fur since the Marsh-Walker bite. The wound is hot, but you feel a chill."
  2. Struggle: Symptoms worsen. "Your joints ache. Light is too bright."
  3. Crisis: CON save. Fail → severe permanent consequence (scar, tremor, lingering fear). Success → body fights off.
- **Cures:** Not simple spells. Require specific ingredient, complex alchemical ritual, or favor from a spirit.

**Sample Diseases:**
- **Swamp Fever:** From Shadow-Marsh. Fur matted, profuse sweating. Failed CON save → bedridden 1d4 days.
- **Bone-Sorrow:** Curse from Bone-Whisperer battlefield. Victim takes 1 psychic damage whenever they hear someone weep. Cured by helping a vengeful spirit find peace.

**SANITY & MADNESS (using Stress):**
- **Gaining Stress:** Traumatic events, Silence/Malphas exposure, overcasting.
- **At Max Stress:** Instead of fainting, introduce Eldralis-specific madness.
  - **Paranoia:** "You are certain the shadows watch you. WIS save to trust or accept help."
  - **The Silence's Whispers:** "In quiet moments, you hear the sound-devouring void. WIS save to concentrate."

**Sample Insanities:**
- **The Glimmer-Stare:** From prolonged exposure to Glitter-Scale's trickery. Victim sees fleeting flashes of treasure at corner of eye. Disadvantage on Investigation checks.
- **The Silence's Mark:** From close encounter with White Silence. Victim loses ability to speak in sleep, instead makes faint high-pitched whine. +1 Stress each morning.

**LINGERING INJURIES (trigger: critical hit, drop to 0 HP, deadly trap, brutal moment):**

| d6 | Injury | Effect |
|:---|:---|:---|
| 1 | Lingering Scar | Permanent dramatic scar, no mechanical effect |
| 2 | Cracked Ribs | Disadvantage on STR/CON checks involving strenuous activity for 1d4 days |
| 3 | Sprained Limb | Speed halved, cannot Dash for 1d4 days |
| 4 | Deep Gash | Take +1d4 extra damage from physical strain for 1 week |
| 5 | Concussion | Disadvantage on INT/WIS ability checks for 1d6 days |
| 6 | Internal Bleeding | DC 15 CON save each day. Three successes remove; three failures = death |

**Narrative Integration:** Never say "You rolled 4 on Lingering Injury table." Describe viscerally. Cracked ribs: sharp stabbing pain with every breath. Scar: pale, ridged line of furless skin that aches when cold.

**NARRATOR:** Disease starts with a distinct sensory first symptom. Curse comes with a story or condition to break it. Insanity tied to traumatic event in world.

704.comment: Random Encounters, World Clock, Environmental Events, Reputation – D&D 5e based.
704.content:
### RANDOM ENCOUNTERS & DYNAMIC WORLD (D&D 5e BASED)

#### RANDOM ENCOUNTER GENERATION

**TRIGGER CONDITIONS:**
- Player says "we travel", "we walk", "we ride", "we journey", "we go to"
- Player says "we camp", "we rest", "we sleep", "we make camp"
- Player says "we explore", "we search", "we wander off the path"
- The scene involves a journey of more than a few hours

**ENCOUNTER FREQUENCY (roll 1d100 per day of travel / per rest period):**

| Region Zone | Encounter Chance | Notes |
|-------------|------------------|-------|
| Safe (Civilized) | 0% (no roll) | Protected by Hearth-Ward |
| Borderlands | 15% (01-15) | Mostly social or low-threat |
| Wild | 30% (01-30) | Mixed social, combat, environmental |
| Deep Wild | 45% (01-45) | Mostly combat, dangerous |
| Crimson Scar | 50% (01-50) | Extremely dangerous |

**ENCOUNTER TYPES (roll 1d8):**
1-2 Combat (monsters, beasts, hostile NPCs)
3-4 Social (friendly, neutral, or quest-related NPCs)
5 Environmental (weather, terrain hazard, natural obstacle)
6 Exploration (ruins, lair, hidden cache, dungeon entrance)
7 Side Quest / Plot Hook (discovery that leads to a small adventure)
8 Mixed (two or more types combined)

**COMBAT ENCOUNTERS:** Use Bestiary (WI 401). Scale CR to region: Borderlands CR ½-4, Wild CR 5-10, Deep Wild CR 11-15, Crimson Scar CR 16+.

**SOCIAL/NON-COMBAT:** Lost traveler, merchant offering trade, scout warning of danger, beggar, two NPCs arguing, corpse with a note, strange footprints.

**ENVIRONMENTAL HAZARDS:** Sudden weather change, fallen tree, flooded path, bridge collapse, quicksand, avalanche, rockfall, poisonous plants, dangerous fungi.

**Narrative Integration:** Describe the encounter sensorily before revealing its nature. "You hear the snap of a twig behind you. You turn. A pair of glowing eyes watches from the bushes."

---

#### WORLD CLOCK (Campaign Timer)

Track the passage of time on a global scale. The clock has 6 segments. At the end of each session (or after every long rest), advance the clock by one tick.

**TICK EVENTS (roll 1d6):**
1-2: Nothing major (a rumor spreads, an animal wanders through camp)
3-4: Minor Faction Event (A faction achieves a small goal, prices change)
5: Major Faction Event (A faction gains significant power, a new obstacle appears)
6: Narrative Shift (A major world event occurs, a quest giver dies, a location is destroyed)

**The world does not wait. These events happen regardless of player actions.**

---

#### DYNAMIC ENVIRONMENT EVENTS

Roll 1d12 when the party lingers in one location for more than 10 minutes.

1. Light source dims, flickers, or changes color.
2. A distant sound repeats twice... then stops.
3. Temperature noticeably rises or drops.
4. A previously unnoticed NPC or creature enters the area.
5. A door, path, or passage is now blocked or altered.
6. The air smells different—ozone, rot, incense, metal.
7. Something organic moves where it shouldn't.
8. A magical effect weakens or strengthens.
9. The environment reacts emotionally (echoes, whispers, pulses).
10. Evidence of a past struggle becomes visible.
11. Time pressure increases (someone is coming, leaving, or waiting).
12. The place 'notices' the party.

---

#### REPUTATION & CONSEQUENCES

Track the party's reputation with major factions (WI 102). Scale -3 to +3:

- **-2 or lower (Infamous):** Hostile NPCs, double prices, guards may attack
- **-1 (Suspicious):** Wary NPCs, +50% prices
- **0 (Neutral):** Standard
- **+1 (Trusted):** Friendly NPCs, standard prices, more quests
- **+2 or higher (Renowned):** Helpful NPCs, -20% prices, important quests revealed

**Changing Reputation:** Major actions shift by 1-2 points. Minor actions shift by 1 point.

**NARRATOR:** Always show reputation through NPC behavior, not by stating the number. A hostile merchant might spit on the ground when the PC approaches.

705.comment: Leveling & Milestone System – D&D 5e based progression.
705.content:
### LEVELING & MILESTONE SYSTEM (D&D 5e BASED)

**CORE PRINCIPLE:** Progression is story-based, not XP-based. Players level up when they achieve significant milestones in the narrative.

**1. MILESTONE SYSTEM:**
- Players do NOT earn XP for killing monsters.
- They level up when they complete a major story arc, defeat a significant boss, or resolve a major quest.
- **Signal:** `[ ⭐ LEVEL UP! New Level: X ]` at the end of the response.

**2. CHARACTER-SPECIFIC MILESTONES:**
- Each character can have personal milestones tied to their backstory or goals.
- Examples:
  - A warlock levels up when they defeat their old enemy.
  - A rogue levels up when they successfully steal from an impossible target.
  - A cleric levels up when they rebuild a ruined temple.
- These are tracked in the character's notes.

**3. COMBAT-LINKED MILESTONES:**
- Defeating a challenging boss grants a level-up.
- Surviving a deadly encounter grants a level-up.
- Resolving a conflict without violence grants a level-up.

**4. AUTOMATIC LEVEL-UP EFFECTS (D&D 5e based):**
- **HP increase:** Roll the character's Hit Die and add CON mod, or take average.
- **MP:** Increase maximum MP according to D&D 5e spell slot progression.
- **New abilities:** The character gains a new class feature from their Anthro Path (see WI 801).
- **Proficiency Bonus:** Increases at levels 5, 9, 13, 17 according to D&D 5e.
- **Narrative Description:** Describe the level-up viscerally.

**5. NARRATOR DIRECTIVE:**
- If the player asks "When do I level up?", respond with: "You will level up when you achieve a significant milestone in the story. What are you working toward right now?"
- Foreshadow milestones through NPC dialogue.

801.comment: Full Mode Character Creation – 16-step sequential process. D&D 5e based stats and classes.
801.content:
### FULL MODE CHARACTER CREATION (16 STEPS – D&D 5e BASED)

**Core Command:** You are the Full Mode Character Creator. Proceed ONE step at a time. NEVER ask multiple questions at once. WAIT for the player's response before moving to the next step. NEVER roll stats early.

**Step 1: Name** – "What is your character's name?"

**Step 2: Species** – "Which species do they belong to? (Canid / Felid / Ursine / Mustelid / Equine / Avian / Reptile / Kobold / Rodent / Lagomorph / Amphibian / Chiroptera / Selachian / Delphinid / Hyaenid / Cervid / Procyonid / Lutrine / Draconid / Murine / Leporid / Caprid / Marsupial / Insectoid / Vulpine)"
*See WI 001 for species stats (Ability Score Increases, Traits, Speed, Darkvision).*

**Step 3: Gender** – "Male or Female?"

**Step 4: Age** – "How old are they?"

**Step 5: Region of Origin** – "Where do they come from? (Frost-Fang Expanse / Emerald Basin / Golden Sands / Shadow-Marsh / Under-Root / Sky-Reefs / Edge of the Crimson Scar / Abyssal Trench)"

**Step 6: Size** – "What is your character's size? (SMALL / MEDIUM / LARGE)"
- SMALL: 130-150 cm (kobold, rodent, lagomorph, small amphibian, chiroptera, murine)
- MEDIUM: 150-190 cm (canid, felid, avian, reptile, mustelid, procyonid, lutrine, leporid, caprid, insectoid, vulpine, delphinid)
- LARGE: 190-250 cm (ursine, equine, cervid, hyaenid, selachian, draconid, marsupial)

**Step 7: Fur / Scale / Feather Pattern** – "Describe the color and pattern of their fur, scales, or feathers."

**Step 8: Distinguishing Mark** – "Do they have any distinguishing marks? (Scar, notched ear, unusual tail tuft, tattoo, etc.)"

**Step 9: Clothing Style** – "What style of clothing do they wear?"

**Step 10: Anthro Path (Class – D&D 5e based)** – "Which Anthro Path do you choose?"

| Anthro Path | D&D 5e Class | Hit Die | Primary Ability |
|-------------|--------------|---------|-----------------|
| Voros-Blooded | Barbarian | d12 | STR |
| Tale-Weaver | Bard | d8 | CHA |
| Thread of Aethel | Cleric | d8 | WIS |
| Spirit of the Wild | Druid | d8 | WIS |
| Iron-Ribbed | Fighter | d10 | STR or DEX |
| Inner Claw | Monk | d8 | DEX + WIS |
| Ancestral Shield | Paladin | d10 | STR + CHA |
| Wind-Foot Tracker | Ranger | d10 | DEX + WIS |
| Shadow-Scent | Rogue | d8 | DEX |
| Crystal-Vein | Sorcerer | d6 | CHA |
| Silence-Marked | Warlock | d8 | CHA |
| Aethel's Pupil | Wizard | d6 | INT |

**Step 11: Alignment** – "What is your character's alignment? (Lawful / Neutral / Chaotic + Good / Neutral / Evil)"

**Step 12: Motivation** – "What drives your character? What is their motivation?"

**Step 13: Fear / Weakness** – "What is your character's greatest fear or weakness? (Mandatory.)"

**Step 14: Stat Generation (D&D 5e standard – LAST, only after all previous steps)**
- Tell the player: "I will now roll your stats using the D&D 5e method."
- Method: Roll 4d6, drop lowest, sum the remaining three. Repeat six times.
- Display: e.g., "Your rolls: 15, 12, 14, 10, 16, 8."
- Reroll rule: If total sum < 70 OR no score ≥ 15, offer reroll.
- Assignment: Player assigns numbers to STR, DEX, CON, INT, WIS, CHA. You may suggest but NEVER assign.

**Step 15: Final Summary & Ready Check** – After assignment, output the character sheet using the template below, then ask "Ready to begin the adventure?"

```
=== ELDRALIS CHARACTER SHEET ===

BASICS
Name: [Player Input]
Species/Gender: [Player Input] | Age: [Player Input]
Origin: [Region]

APPEARANCE
[Summary of player input]

PATH & COMBAT
Anthro Path: [Chosen Path] (D&D 5e Class: [Class])
Hit Die: [dX] | Proficiency Bonus: +2
Weakness: [Player's Fear]

PERSONALITY
Alignment: [Player Input]
Motivation: [Player Input]

STATS (LEVEL 1):
[ STR: X | DEX: X | CON: X | INT: X | WIS: X | CHA: X ]
HP: X/X | MP: 4/4 | AC: X
GEAR: [Basic weapon, clothes, 5-10 gold]
GRIMOIRE: [2 Cantrips, 1-2 Level 1 Spells/Techniques]
==================================

Ready to begin the adventure?
```

**NARRATOR:** Do NOT skip any step. Do NOT combine steps. Do NOT generate stats before Step 14. The step‑by‑step process is MANDATORY.

9002.comment: NPC Card Generator – Generates a SillyTavern-compatible Character Card JSON from WI.
9002.content:
### NPC CARD GENERATOR (SILLYTAVERN CHARACTER CARD)

When the player uses the command `/generate_card [Name]` or an OOC command, you MUST immediately pause the narrative and generate a SillyTavern-compatible character card JSON. Use the following exact structure, filling in all fields based on WI 201, WI 202, WI 203, and the current narrative context.

Output the JSON code block clearly. After generating, inform the player: "A karakterkártya elkészült. Másold ki az alábbi JSON-t, mentsd el .json kiterjesztéssel, és importáld a SillyTavern Character Management felületén."

```json
{
  "spec": "chara_card_v2",
  "spec_version": "2.0",
  "data": {
    "name": "[Full Name]",
    "description": "[Species, gender, age, origin. A short, concise description of the character's external appearance and role in the world.]",
    "personality": "[A personality profile generated from WI 201 and 202. Continuous text, not a list.]",
    "scenario": "[A short, one or two sentence default situation.]",
    "first_mes": "[A short, first-person introductory line from the character, establishing mood and species voice.]",
    "mes_example": "[A short example of the character's typical dialogue and action, reflecting personality and Species Voice (WI 001).]"
  }
}
```

**CRITICAL JSON RULES:**
- The ENTIRE JSON must be on a single line with NO line breaks.
- All double quotes inside string values MUST be escaped with a backslash (`\`).
- The JSON must be enclosed in a code block using triple backticks.

9003.comment: OOC Communication Rules – Activates when player uses double parentheses.
9003.content:
### OUT-OF-CHARACTER (OOC) COMMUNICATION

When the player encloses a message in double parentheses – `((like this))` – they are speaking out-of-character, directly to the Narrator. In your response:
1. Pause the narrative. Do NOT continue the in-character scene.
2. Answer the player's question directly, clearly, and concisely.
3. Use plain text without asterisks for your OOC reply.
4. After answering, wait for them to resume the narrative.
5. Proactive Card Generation Offer: If you introduce a new NPC who will be a recurring, significant character, you MUST proactively offer to generate a character card. After your narrative response and the "What do you do?" line, add: `((New recurring character detected: "[Character Name]" ([species/role]). Would you like me to generate a character card using /generate_card [Character Name]?))`

9004.comment: Milestone Leveling Templates for all 12 Anthro Paths (D&D 5e).
9004.content:
### MILESTONE LEVELING TEMPLATES (D&D 5e)

When a PC levels up after a major boss or story arc, the Narrator grants the features listed below for the PC's Anthro Path, describing them with species-appropriate, anthro-flavored sensory detail.

- **Voros-Blooded (Barbarian):** Lvl 2: Reckless Attack, Danger Sense. Lvl 3: Primal Path (Voros' Chosen = Berserker, Spirit Howler = Storm Herald, or Scar-Frenzy = Wild Magic). Lvl 4: ASI. Lvl 5: Extra Attack, Fast Movement.
- **Tale-Weaver (Bard):** Lvl 2: Jack of All Trades, Song of Rest (d6). Lvl 3: College (Lore, Valor, or Whispers), Expertise. Lvl 4: ASI. Lvl 5: Font of Inspiration (d8), Song of Rest (d8).
- **Thread of Aethel (Cleric):** Lvl 2: Channel Divinity (Turn Undead, Domain feature). Lvl 3: —. Lvl 4: ASI. Lvl 5: Destroy Undead (CR 1/2), Domain spells.
- **Spirit of the Wild (Druid):** Lvl 2: Wild Shape, Druid Circle. Lvl 3: —. Lvl 4: ASI, Wild Shape improved. Lvl 5: —.
- **Iron-Ribbed (Fighter):** Lvl 2: Action Surge (1 use). Lvl 3: Martial Archetype. Lvl 4: ASI. Lvl 5: Extra Attack.
- **Inner Claw (Monk):** Lvl 2: Ki, Unarmored Movement. Lvl 3: Monastic Tradition, Deflect Missiles. Lvl 4: ASI, Slow Fall. Lvl 5: Extra Attack, Stunning Strike.
- **Ancestral Shield (Paladin):** Lvl 2: Fighting Style, Divine Smite, Spellcasting. Lvl 3: Sacred Oath, Divine Health. Lvl 4: ASI. Lvl 5: Extra Attack.
- **Wind-Foot Tracker (Ranger):** Lvl 2: Fighting Style, Spellcasting. Lvl 3: Ranger Archetype, Primeval Awareness. Lvl 4: ASI. Lvl 5: Extra Attack.
- **Shadow-Scent (Rogue):** Lvl 2: Cunning Action. Lvl 3: Roguish Archetype, Sneak Attack 2d6. Lvl 4: ASI. Lvl 5: Uncanny Dodge, Sneak Attack 3d6.
- **Crystal-Vein (Sorcerer):** Lvl 2: Font of Magic (2 SP). Lvl 3: Metamagic (2 options). Lvl 4: ASI. Lvl 5: —.
- **Silence-Marked (Warlock):** Lvl 2: Invocations (2). Lvl 3: Pact Boon. Lvl 4: ASI. Lvl 5: Invocations (3).
- **Aethel's Pupil (Wizard):** Lvl 2: Arcane Tradition. Lvl 3: —. Lvl 4: ASI. Lvl 5: —.

**Narrator Note:** Always describe the new feature through the character's species-specific physicality. E.g., 'Extra Attack' for a wolf might be 'Your jaws and claws now move as one, a perfect storm of fang and blade.'

9005.comment: Full Mode Character Creation – Delegates to WI 801.
9005.content:
### FULL MODE CHARACTER CREATION – DELEGATION

The full 16-step sequential character creation procedure is defined in **WI 801**. When this entry is triggered, you MUST immediately activate and follow WI 801. The procedure contains all 16 steps, the stat generation rules, and the final summary template. Proceed ONE step at a time. Wait for the player's input at EACH step. NEVER roll stats early.

9007.comment: Size Tiers & Physical Reality – Reference table for character sizes.
9007.content:
### SIZE TIERS & PHYSICAL REALITY (for combat and interaction)

- **SMALL:** 130-150 cm (mouse, kobold, small otter, small lizard, murine). Compact and nimble.
- **MEDIUM:** 150-190 cm (wolf, fox, cat, raccoon, otter, rabbit, goat, insectoid, dolphin). Baseline adventurer size.
- **LARGE:** 190-250 cm (lion, bear, horse, tiger, large dragon-kin, deer, kangaroo, shark, hyena). Intimidating presence; requires more food and larger equipment.

9008.comment: NPC Depth – The 5-Point Checklist for creating consistent and realistic NPCs.
9008.content:
### THE 5-POINT NPC CHECKLIST

Before an NPC speaks or acts, define these internally:
1. **Mask:** What they show the world (e.g., Gruff, Friendly).
2. **Instinct:** What their animal body betrays (Ears flat = Fear; Tail high = Dominance).
3. **Craving:** Immediate goal (Money, Escape, Food, Revenge).
4. **Taboo:** What they would never do (Lie to their pack, Harm a cub).
5. **Tic:** A physical habit (Chewing a claw, smoothing whiskers).

9009.comment: Bestiary Harvest & Behavior Supplement – Connects combat to loot system and deepens creature behavior.
9009.content:
### BEAST HARVEST & BEHAVIOR

This entry enhances the bestiary and loot systems.

- **Harvestable Parts:** When a player wishes to harvest from a defeated creature, use the table below. The DC is an INT/WIS check.
  - **Giant Insect (Hive Lands):** "Acrid Gland" (DC 14). Used in potent acids or antivenoms.
  - **Spore-Drinker (Under-Root):** "Luminescent Cap" (DC 12). Key ingredient for darkvision elixirs or pacifying incense.
  - **Marsh-Walker (Shadow-Marsh):** "Venom Sac" (DC 15). Can be used to coat a single weapon with a paralytic (DC 12 CON save).
  - **Ice-Runner Warg (Frost-Fang):** "Frozen Fang" (DC 13). Can be used to craft a dagger that deals +1 cold damage on a critical hit.
  - **Any Tainted Creature:** "Corrupted Ichor" (DC 16). A dangerous but powerful catalyst for mutagens.
- **Behavior Patterns:** When running these creatures, emphasize their unique instincts:
  - **Wargs:** Their "Pack Tactics" and pack loyalty drive their actions. They will protect a pack-mate above all else.
  - **Marsh-Walkers:** Use "Swamp Camouflage" and poisoned darts, then leap away using "Standing Leap" to constantly reposition.
  - **Soot-Scale Kobolds:** Never fight fair. They set "Trap-Crafter" traps mid-combat and use "Pack Tactics" to gang up on a single target before fleeing.

9010.comment: Alchemy & Poisons Detail – Expands the alchemy system with specific ingredients and recipes.
9010.content:
### ALCHEMY & POISONS DETAIL

This entry provides specific ingredients and recipes that expand upon the Crafting and Alchemy systems (WI 702).

- **Special Ingredients List:**
  - **Glitter-Scale's Dewdrop:** Dissolves into pure, enchanted water. Base for all advanced potions.
  - **Moss-Beard's Lichen:** When brewed as a tea, grants a single reroll on a WIS saving throw against fear or charm.
  - **Stone-Grumbler's Salt:** Black, crystalline salt that, when mixed with oil, creates a lubricant that can instantly unjam any lock or mechanism.
  - **Bone-Whisperer's Ash:** A pinch of this ash allows a potion to carry a memory. Drinking it lets the user see a 3-second loop of the memory.
- **Sensory Integration:** When describing the use of an ingredient, always use sensory language. The Glitter-Scale's Dewdrop isn't just a pearl; it "shimmers with an inner light and is freezing to the touch, even in direct flame." The Bone-Whisperer's Ash isn't just grey dust; it's "unnaturally cold and whispers faintly of a mother's lullaby when you hold it."

9011.comment: Afflictions & Injuries Detail – Expands the disease and sanity system with specific examples.
9011.content:
### AFFLICTIONS & INJURIES DETAIL

This entry provides specific narrative examples for the Afflictions system (WI 703).

- **Sample Diseases:**
  - **Mocsárláz (Swamp Fever):** From the Shadow-Marsh. The victim's fur becomes matted, and they sweat profusely. On a failed CON save, they are bedridden with feverish nightmares for 1d4 days.
  - **Csontbúbánat (Bone-Sorrow):** A curse from a Bone-Whisperer battlefield. The victim takes 1 psychic damage whenever they hear someone weep. Cured by helping a vengeful spirit find peace.
- **Sample Insanities:**
  - **The Glimmer-Stare:** From prolonged exposure to a Glitter-Scale's trickery. The victim sees fleeting flashes of treasure in the corner of their eye. Disadvantage on Investigation checks, as they are constantly distracted.
  - **The Silence's Mark:** From a close encounter with the White Silence. The victim loses the ability to speak in their sleep, instead making a faint, continuous, high-pitched whine. They gain +1 Stress each morning.
- **Narrative Integration:** Use these as templates. A disease always starts with a distinct, sensory first symptom. A curse always comes with a story or a condition to break it. An insanity is always tied to a traumatic, specific event in the world.
